name: Tour from Aiguillon ending at Croix du Pech-de-Berre
name_original: Tour depuis Aiguillon se terminant à la Croix du Pech-de-Berre
description: This moderate 38.7-mile (62.3 km) road cycling route from Aiguillon offers panoramic views from Croix du Pech-de-Berre, with challenging fin
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aiguillon are characterized by the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features tranquil river valleys, rolling countryside, and gentle hills, with routes often following small country lanes. Cyclists can explore areas with orchards and waterways, providing varied scenery. The landscape is predominantly low-lying, with minimal significant climbs. Best road cycling routes around Aiguillon The most popular road cycling route is Tour from Aiguillon ending at Croix du Pech-de-Berre, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Tonneins loop from Aiguillon, a moderate 48.2 miles (77.6 km) path. This route provides expansive views across the Lot Valley. Local road cyclists also love the Bridge over the Baïse in Vianne – Nérac loop from Aiguillon, a 52.9 miles (85.1 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past historic villages, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Aiguillon is defined by river confluences, tranquil valleys, and canals. The network offers options for easy rides and more challenging routes with varied elevation. The routes in Aiguillon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aiguillon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find in Aiguillon?
A: Aiguillon offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, characterized by the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers. You'll find tranquil river valleys, rolling countryside, and gentle hills. Routes often follow small country lanes, passing through areas with orchards and waterways. The landscape is predominantly low-lying, with minimal significant climbs, making it suitable for various skill levels.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aiguillon?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Aiguillon documented on komoot, catering to different preferences and abilities. These include 2 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 7 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Aiguillon?
A: Yes, Aiguillon has several routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride. The region's predominantly low-lying terrain and gentle hills make it accessible. You can find 2 easy routes, and many of the 31 moderate routes also offer a comfortable cycling experience on quiet country lanes.

Q: What are some scenic road cycling routes that offer viewpoints or natural features?
A: For scenic views, consider routes that follow the Lot and Garonne rivers. The region is known for its picturesque waterways and rolling countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36298897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tonneins loop from Aiguillon&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views across the Lot Valley. You can also explore routes along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a different perspective of the local waterways.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling trails around Aiguillon?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Aiguillon are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36298897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tonneins loop from Aiguillon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5365733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Baïse in Vianne – Nérac loop from Aiguillon&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through riverine landscapes and past historic villages.

Q: What kind of attractions or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you can encounter various attractions. Routes often pass by historic villages, old farms, dovecotes, and châteaux. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Christopher's Church in Vianne&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buzet-sur-Baïse Lock&lt;/a&gt;. The region's rich maritime heritage is also visible around the Port de l'Aiguillon.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Aiguillon?
A: The road cycling routes in Aiguillon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Over 700 road cyclists have explored the area, often praising the quiet country lanes, diverse riverine and countryside scenery, and the well-maintained network of routes that cater to various abilities.

Q: Are there any specific routes that follow canals or rivers?
A: Absolutely. Aiguillon's location at the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers means many routes incorporate these waterways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35611206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Canal view loop from Aiguillon&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for cycling alongside the canal, offering a unique perspective of the local environment.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aiguillon?
A: Aiguillon offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm, but routes along rivers and canals often offer shade. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for advanced cyclists?
A: Yes, for more advanced cyclists looking for longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5365733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Baïse in Vianne – Nérac loop from Aiguillon&lt;/a&gt;, at 85.1 km, provide a substantial challenge. While the region is generally low-lying, some routes include varied elevation to test your endurance.

Q: Can I connect to broader cycling networks from Aiguillon?
A: Yes, Aiguillon is strategically located to connect with broader cycling networks. The Lot Valley Cycle Route leads along winding country lanes to medieval villages, and Aiguillon can also link into the extensive Canal des 2 Mers Cycle Route, offering opportunities for longer touring adventures.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Lot Valley Cycle Route?
A: For more detailed information about the Lot Valley Cycle Route, including sections connecting Aiguillon to Villeneuve-sur-Lot, you can visit &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/lot-valley-cycle-route/aiguillon-villeneuve-sur-lot" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;France Vélo Tourisme&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: How long does the racebike tour from Aiguillon to Croix du Pech-de-Berre typically take?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 47 minutes to complete. This duration is based on the tour's distance of about 62.3 km and moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this tour in Aiguillon?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations for cyclists in Aiguillon are not detailed, it's generally advisable to look for public parking areas within the town center or near the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, which is a common starting point for routes in the area. Consider checking local tourism websites like tourisme-lotetgaronne.com for up-to-date information on facilities.

faqs.question: Is this racebike tour suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This tour is rated as moderate. While initial sections from Aiguillon may involve paved roads, the final ascent to the Croix du Pech-de-Berre can be quite challenging for traditional racebikes. It transitions from paved roads to wide farm tracks and then to narrower, steep, and sometimes technical paths. Some segments might even require carrying your bike, making it less suitable for absolute beginners on a racebike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the "Tour from Aiguillon ending at Croix du Pech-de-Berre"?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly. The initial parts likely feature paved roads, possibly including a descent along the river. However, as you approach the Pech-de-Berre, the terrain becomes considerably more challenging. Expect strong climbs, transitions from paved roads to wide farm tracks, and then to narrower, technical paths. Some sections can be very steep, and you might encounter segments that are very narrow or disappear, potentially requiring you to dismount and carry your bike.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to undertake this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: The Lot-et-Garonne region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring to early autumn. Spring provides lush greenery and comfortable temperatures, while early autumn offers cooler weather and beautiful foliage. Summer can be hot, so starting early in the day is recommended if cycling during July or August.

faqs.question: What scenic views and landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The tour offers beautiful scenery, including a Canal view. The highlight of the route is undoubtedly The Croix du Pech-de-Berre and Panoramic View. From the summit of Pech-de-Berre, you'll be rewarded with expansive 360-degree panoramas encompassing the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, fertile valleys, the beginnings of the Landes forest, and the hills of Pays de Serres and Gascogne.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog allowances for this racebike tour is not available. Given the nature of road cycling and potentially challenging terrain on the final ascent, it might not be ideal for dogs, especially off-leash. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely accompany you on a bike or check local regulations for paths and roads.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to undertake this racebike tour. The roads and paths used are generally public access.

faqs.question: Does this tour intersect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly overlaps with sections of several other cycling paths, including the Circuit Rembrandt, Circuit des Vignobles de Buzet, Circuit entre Garonne et Canal, and the Circuit des Cadets de Gascogne. It also touches upon the EV3 France 42, Langon - Buzet-sur-Baïse, and the Véloroute de la Vallée du Lot.

faqs.question: What is the main challenge for racebikes on this tour, especially towards the Croix du Pech-de-Berre?
faqs.answer: The primary challenge for racebikes lies in the final ascent to the Croix du Pech-de-Berre. While the initial parts of the tour are on paved roads, the path to the summit involves strong climbs, transitions to wide farm tracks, and then to narrower, technical, and very steep paths. These sections are often not suitable for traditional racebike tires and gearing, potentially requiring cyclists to dismount and carry their bikes for short stretches.

faqs.question: Is the Tour from Aiguillon ending at Croix du Pech-de-Berre a loop trail?
faqs.answer: No, this tour is described as 'ending at Croix du Pech-de-Berre,' indicating it is an out-and-back or point-to-point route. You would need to plan your return journey from the Croix du Pech-de-Berre back to Aiguillon or arrange for transport.
